guideposts.label: Welcome to the live coverage of stage 17 of the Tour de France 2026
guideposts.detail: After the time trial ruled by Remco Evenepoel, the peloton return to action with a 174.7 km stage from Chambéry to Voiron on rolling terrain. Can sprinters resits baroudeurs? Or will attackers prevail? The stage packs 2,200 metres of elevation gain, but most of the uphill challenges are in the first part of the route. The peloton are set to roll at 13:20, to reach km 0 at 13:35. The winners will b

guideposts.label: A home start for Decathlon CMA CGM
guideposts.detail: Born in 1992 under the guidance of Vincent Lavenu, Decathlon CMA CGM remains true to its Savoyard roots although it has undergone a metamorphosis over the last few years. The team was established in Chambéry and has been based there ever since, but it now describes itself as “international and based in the French Alps”. The oldest French squad in the peloton has had three previous main sponsors: C

guideposts.label: Homegrown talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…
guideposts.detail: Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team. The team has supported the rise of ma

guideposts.label: Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech)
A third start from Chambéry
guideposts.detail: Chambéry hosts a stage start for the third time after 1996 and 2010. The city also saw Rigoberto Uran win stage 9 of the Tour 2017. Chambéry was also the host of the 1989 Road World Championship that crowned Greg LeMond and Jeannie Longo. The Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift stopped in Chambéry in 2025: Maeva Squiban and Cédrine Kerbaol achieved a French 1-2.

guideposts.label: Green jersey
guideposts.detail: No change yesterday: Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) enters the final week with a margin of 31 points on Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech): 417 points against 386. Biniam Girmay (NSN) completes the top 3 of the points standings (361 points). Today’s stage offers up to 75 points: 25 for the first rider at the intermediate sprint (Colombe, km 147.5) and 50 for the stage winner.

guideposts.label: Team classification
guideposts.detail: Lidl-Trek still lead the overall team standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 23’’. Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe are in third place overall (+46’13’’).

guideposts.label: Stage 17 of the Tour de France 2026 is ON!
guideposts.detail: The 164-man peloton just passed km 0.

guideposts.label: Veistroffer is the first attacker of the day
guideposts.detail: He's followed by Abrahamsen.

guideposts.label: A lead duo and many chasers
guideposts.detail: The first attackers of the day, Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché) and Jonas Abrahamsen (Uno-X Mobility), still lead the way at km 6. Kasper Asgreeen (EF Education-EasyPost) and Marco Haller (Tudor) trail by 15 seconds. The peloton are 5 seconds behind them.

guideposts.label: The race is in Aix-les-Bains
guideposts.detail: At the bottom of the Alps, Aix-les-Bains has hosted 21 stage starts and 22 finishes since the Tour first stopped there, in 1931. Notably, the city has witnessed Raymond Poulidor’s first Tour stage win, on 12 July 1962. This edition, exactly 64 years on the day after this maiden victory, Poulidor’s grandson, Mathieu Van der Poel (Alpecin-Premier Tech) won in Ussel.

guideposts.label: A very strecthed bunch in Aix-les-Bains
guideposts.detail: Some splits appear as many riders want to make the break. The chasers are caught. Kévin Vauquelin (Netcompany Ineos) accelerates. Davide Ballerini (XDS Astana) gets back up after a crash. Florian Vermeersch (UAE Emirates XRG) stops because of a mechanical.

guideposts.label: Jonas Abrahamsen and Baptiste Veistroffer
Davide Ballerini est tombé dans un rond-point
New moves at the front
guideposts.detail: Jonas Abrahamsen (Uno-X Mobility) is riding alone at the front at km 15. Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché) is caught by a handful of chasers, including Ben Healy (EF Education-EasyPost), Julian Alaphilippe (Tudor)... The peloton are right behind.

guideposts.label: The green jersey and the polka-dot jersey on the move
guideposts.detail: Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) was the first to close the gap, and now Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) accelerates towards the summit.

guideposts.label: Paret-Peintre takes the first KOM point of the day
guideposts.detail: Alaphilippe counter-attacks after the summit. The balle for the break goes on.

guideposts.label: 20 kilometres of battle
guideposts.detail: Quinn Simmons (Lidl-Trek), Ben O'Connor (Jayco AlUla) and Harold Tejada (XDS Astana) lead the way. Ewen Costiou (Groupama-FDJ United) and Pablo Castrillo (Movistar) drive the chase right behind them. Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché) is off the back.

guideposts.label: Attacks keep flying
guideposts.detail: Counter-attackers just nullified a move from Thymen Arensman (Netcompany Ineos), Xabier Mikel Azparren (Pinarello Q36.5), Pavel Bittner (Picnic PostNL) and Ewen Costiou (Groupama-FDJ United). Mathias Vacek (Lidl-Trek) sets off at km 22.5. Max Walker (EF Education-EasyPost) chases him.

guideposts.label: Pidcock and Martinez involved in a crash
guideposts.detail: Wright was also caught up. Everyone rapidly got back up.

guideposts.label: A crash in the peloton
3 leaders
guideposts.detail: Max Walker (EF Education-EasyPost), Harold Tejada (XDS Astana), and Ion Izagirre (Cofidis) have opened a gap of 15 seconds at km 30. Julian Alaphilippe (Tudor) tried to bridge the gap but he's been reeled in by the bunch. Liam Slock (Lotto Intermarché) sets off.

guideposts.label: Three riders are trying to break away again
3 + 2 = 5 leaders
guideposts.detail: Anthony Turgis (Total Energies) and then Ben Healy (EF Education- EsyPost) join the lead group. The peloton only trail by 10'' at 33.

guideposts.label: Second climb of the day
guideposts.detail: Will the attackers make a difference on the Côte de Rossillon (1.7 km at 5.3%)?

guideposts.label: Counter-attackers close the gap in the final kilometre of ascent...
guideposts.detail: And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-Easypost) goes first at the summit!

guideposts.label: Pedersen launches a new move
guideposts.detail: The green jersey holder is followed by many attackers: Jorgenson, Matthews, Tarling, Garcia Pierna, Denz...

guideposts.label: Around 20 riders on the move
guideposts.detail: Van der Poel is up there with Simmons, Jorgenson, Quinn, Stuyven, Martin-Guyonnet, Braz Afonso... Counter-attackers feel the threat.

guideposts.label: The battle for the polka-dot continues
44.5 kilometres in the first hour
guideposts.detail: The attackers try to maintain a strong pace to get away from the bunch, currently trailing by 10 seconds.

guideposts.label: A large group of around twenty riders has opened up a gap of about twenty seconds on the peloton
22 attackers: Van der Poel, Jorgenson, Simmons...
guideposts.detail: According to Capgemini's trackers, here's the group composition: UAE Emirates XRG: Politt Visma-Lease a Bike: Hagenes, Jorgenson Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe: Denz Lidl-Trek: Simmons EF Education-EasyPost: Baudin, Quinn Decathlon CMA CGM: Benoot XDS Astana: Tejada Bahrain Victorious: Mohoric Netcompany Ineos: Bernal, Tarling Soudal Quick-Step: Stuyven Alpecin-Premier Tech: Van der Poel Jayco AlUla: Eng

guideposts.label: Pedersen reacts
guideposts.detail: As the gap increases to 30 seconds with Alpecin-Premier Tech setting the pace in the bunch, Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) counter attacks. He's followed by a handful of riders.

guideposts.label: Where are the top 10 riders on GC?
guideposts.detail: According to Capgemini’s trackers, Remco Evenepoel (2nd), Paul Seixas (4th), Juan Ayuso (5th), and Mattias Skjelmose (6th) are in the front group with Tadej Pogacar. But Isaac del Toro (3rd), Tom Pidcock (8th), Jordan Jegat (9th) and Yannis Voisard (10th) are in the second group, trailing by 25 seconds. Lenny Martinez (7th) is no longer tracked after he swapped bikes due to a crash.

guideposts.label: On the attack towards the high point of the race
Martinez is in the first group; Del Toro is not
guideposts.detail: The gap as the peloton returns to Chambéry: 40 seconds.

guideposts.label: Tarling attacks
guideposts.detail: Simmons chases him. The front group explodes again, with the GC contenders at the back. They wait for the Del Toro group.

guideposts.label: Biniam Girmay is not in the green jersey peloton
Halfway through the stage
guideposts.detail: The sixteen attackers struggle to work well together. In the first bunch, Decathlon CMA CGM control the gap at around 40 seconds. Several teams work together in the Girmay group: NSN, XDS Astana, Picnic PostNL, Netcompany Ineos...

guideposts.label: The pace drops in the bunch...
guideposts.detail: Kasper Asgreen (EF Education-EasyPost) immediately tries to launch a new move but he's closely tracked.

guideposts.label: 75 kilometres to go for the 16 leaders
guideposts.detail: +45'' Jorgenson, Healy and Le Berre try to close the gap. +1'15'' Hoole is back at the front of the bunch to set the pace with Abrahamsen. +3'05'' The Girmay group lose terrain.

guideposts.label: The simply magnificent scenery
The chasers are closing the gap
guideposts.detail: They trail by a dozen seconds with 69 kilometres to go. The gap to the bunch is also decreasing: 45 seconds.

guideposts.label: Jorgenson, Healy and Le Berre join the breakaway
guideposts.detail: That makes a group of 19 leaders with 68 kilometres left. The peloton trail by 30 secodns. The Girmay group are 1'55'' further behind.

guideposts.label: The situation settles? Pedersen attacks!
guideposts.detail: The green jersey wearer wants to bridge the 20-second gap alone. He is 65 kilometres from the finish.

guideposts.label: Pedersen joins the front group
guideposts.detail: He immediately gives a fresh impetus to the breakaway. The gap to the bunch increases to 35'' with 63 km to go. Many riders counter-attack.

special_markers.label: Pidcock and Martinez involved in a crash
